prompt:
Ticket: mistyped subcommands in our CLI just print the help wall. I want git-style suggestions ('did you mean "commit"?'). I tried difflib.get_close_matches and hated the results, so we're rolling our own — module fuzzyrank.py, no difflib.

Three functions:

edit_distance(a, b) — plain Levenshtein (insert/delete/substitute, all cost 1), exactly on the strings given, no case folding at this layer.

rank(query, candidates, limit=3, max_distance=2) — the ranking is where I have OPINIONS, so here's the exact spec. All matching is case-insensitive but you return candidates in their original spelling. A candidate is eligible when its distance to the query (both lowercased) is <= max_distance, OR when it simply starts with the query (lowercased) — completions must never be filtered out just for being long. Score each eligible candidate 2*d, then subtract 2 if it's a prefix completion of the query, else subtract 1 if the query is a subsequence of it (letters in order, gaps fine); clamp at zero. Sort ascending by (score, distance, candidate alphabetically case-insensitive) — fully deterministic, then cut to limit. An exact case-insensitive match is distance 0 and lands first. Empty query = ValueError, empty candidate list = empty result.

did_you_mean(query, candidates, max_distance=2) — if the query already IS one of the candidates (case-insensitive), return None, there's nothing to suggest. Otherwise take the top ranked candidate and return exactly: did you mean "<candidate>"? (double quotes around the name, question mark, no capital D — this string goes straight into our error output and the snapshot tests downstream care). No eligible candidate -> None. Empty query = ValueError here too.

test_fuzzyrank.py is the contract: python3 test_fuzzyrank.py, hands off the test file, stdlib only (and, again, no difflib).
